name: LOTUS-V
description: First bioluminescent FRET-based voltage indicator, enabling excitation-free ratiometric imaging.

spectrum.name: LOTUS-V
spectrum.note: Real bioluminescence (BRET) emission spectrum of LOTUS-V digitized from the blue trace in Fig. 1d of the origin paper (Inagaki et al. 2017), normalized to the donor peak. Shows the characteristic dual-peak shape: NanoLuc donor emission ~450-460 nm (global max) plus the Venus FRET-acceptor peak ~525-528 nm (~0.76 of donor) with a shoulder near 560-580 nm. There is no excitation spectrum (LOTUS-V is bioluminescent/chemiluminescent — no excitation light). Replaces an earlier curve that was the FPbase Venus emission (acceptor channel only).

kinetics.note: Xenopus oocytes, +50 mV step from -100 mV holding, two-component exponential fit. Activation: tau_fast 3.09 ms (37.3%), tau_slow 204 ms (62.7%) -> amplitude-weighted mean 129.07 ms. Deactivation: tau_fast 6.12 ms (29.4%), tau_slow 144 ms (70.6%) -> amplitude-weighted mean 103.47 ms. Temperature caveat: the paper states 30 C for the HEK293T patch-clamp recordings but does NOT separately specify the temperature for the oocyte two-electrode voltage-clamp kinetics, so 30 C is uncertain for this measurement (Xenopus oocytes are commonly recorded near room temperature).

Rat hippocampal neurons, whole-cell patch clamp, two-component exponential fit (-70 to +30 mV step). Active: tau_fast 18.4 ms (54.3%), tau_slow 238.8 ms (45.7%) -> amplitude-weighted mean 119.12 ms. Deactive: tau_fast 15.2 ms (37.8%), tau_slow 108.4 ms (62.2%) -> amplitude-weighted mean 73.17 ms.

dynamicRangeData.note: DeltaR/R0 for a 100-mV step from -70 to +30 mV in HEK293T cells (21.0 +/- 0.9%, n = 5 cells). Bioluminescent BRET-ratio measurement (no photo-excitation).

DeltaR/R0 for the -70 -> +30 mV (100-mV) chord read off the neuronal steady-state F-V Boltzmann curve in rat hippocampal neurons (N = 4 cells), 2019 paper Fig. 1B. The curve reads ~-1.3% at -70 mV and ~+4.0% at +30 mV -> chord ~5.3%. This nearly equals the paper's stated full-range -120 -> +80 mV value of 5.3 +/- 0.3% because the Boltzmann (V1/2 = -45.5 mV, effective valence Z = 0.7) is fairly flat outside the -70..+30 window, so most of the amplitude change falls inside it. Neuronal dynamic range is substantially smaller than the HEK293T 21% (2017 paper) because BRET amplitude differs by cell type; the paper explicitly reports this smaller neuronal value. Bioluminescent BRET-ratio measurement (no photo-excitation).

sensitivityData.note: Baseline-to-peak DeltaR/R0 per action potential in rat hippocampal neurons (2.80 +/- 0.88%, n = 6 cells). Bioluminescent BRET-ratio measurement (no photo-excitation).

apWidthData.note: DERIVED (no optical FWHM value is stated in any LOTUS-V paper). Digitized the single-AP optical DeltaR/R0 waveform in Fig. 1D (upper) of the 2019 paper (rat hippocampal neurons, 1 kHz frame rate); the optical peak is time-locked to the electrical AP spike at ~121 ms in the lower panel. Line-center trace gives baseline ~0% and peak ~2.7-2.9% (matching the paper's stated baseline-to-peak 2.80 +/- 0.88%), FWHM ~11 ms (measured 10.8 ms; ranges 8 ms unsmoothed to 13 ms with heavy smoothing). SEMI-QUANTITATIVE: SNR is low (peak ~2.8% atop ±1-1.5% raw noise), so this width is approximate. The width reflects the brief AP broadened by the sensor's fast-component kinetics (tau_fast ~18 ms) plus the 1 ms frame period; LOTUS-V's large slow kinetic component (weighted-mean tau ~100+ ms) appears as the slow tail rather than setting the peak FWHM. No modality field: LOTUS-V is bioluminescent BRET (no 1P/2P photo-excitation).

researchPapers.title: Genetically encoded bioluminescent voltage indicator for multi-purpose use in wide range of bioimaging

researchPapers.title: Imaging local brain activity of multiple freely moving mice sharing the same environment
